tavily.answer: Recent developments in Jamaica following Hurricane Melissa indicate ongoing conflict, security, and humanitarian efforts. The government, in collaboration with the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO), is implementing a $50 million agriculture recovery and resilience program set to be fully operational in September, aimed at supporting farmers and fishers affected by the hurricane. Opposition Spokesman on Agriculture and Fisheries, Dr Dayton Campbell, has highlighted the need for a structured youth in agriculture pipeline, advocating for educational, training, and land access initiatives to rejuvenate the sector. The hurricane's impact has also prompted Verizon to assist families in staying connected, reflecting broader efforts to support recovery and resilience in the region.

items.title: Powerful 6.0 earthquake rattles Eastern Caribbean - Jamaica Observer
items.snippet: BRIDGETOWN, Barbados (CMC) – Several islands in the eastern Caribbean were rocked by a 6.0 magnitude earthquake Saturday morning . The epicenter was measured about 43.5 miles from the village of Codrington on the island of Barbuda around 10:50 am (local time).
